name: typescript-eslint (TypeScript-aware ESLint)

performance.notes: Type-aware rules require TypeScript program instantiation — slower than standard ESLint

agent_readiness.notes: TypeScript-aware ESLint integration. npm i -D @typescript-eslint/parser @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin. eslint.config.mjs (flat config): import tseslint from 'typescript-eslint'. export default tseslint.config({ extends: [...tseslint.configs.recommended], languageOptions: { parserOptions: { project: true, tsconfigRootDir: import.meta.dirname } } }). Type-aware rules: @typescript-eslint/no-floating-promises, @typescript-eslint/no-misused-promises, @typescript-eslint/await-thenable, @typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-assignment. Non-type-aware: @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any, @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars. Configs: tseslint.configs.recommended, .recommendedTypeChecked, .strict, .strictTypeChecked. Typed linting requires parserOptions.project = true (slower). Flat config recommended (v7+). Custom rules via plugin.

gotchas: Type-aware linting (project: true) significantly slows linting
v7 uses flat config — legacy .eslintrc requires different setup
Some rules have false positives in complex generic code
parserOptions.project must point to correct tsconfig.json
Oxlint and Biome cover some rules faster but without type awareness
best_when: TypeScript projects needing type-aware linting (floating promises, unsafe assignments)
Enforcing TypeScript best practices beyond what tsc checks
Existing ESLint setups needing TypeScript support
Large TypeScript codebases where type safety is critical
avoid_when: Speed is critical and type-aware rules aren't needed (use Biome or oxlint)
Very large monorepos where tsc project references are complex
summary: typescript-eslint adds TypeScript support to ESLint. import tseslint; tseslint.config({ extends: [tseslint.configs.recommendedTypeChecked], languageOptions: {parserOptions: {project: true}} }). Type-aware rules: no-floating-promises, no-misused-promises, no-unsafe-assignment. MIT. Flat config in v7+. Type-aware rules slow — parserOptions.project instantiates TS program. Essential for TypeScript best practices. Biome/oxlint are faster for non-type-aware rules.
